**Subject:** Thank You – Internship Program Completion

Dear Professor [Name],

I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to express my sincere appreciation for hosting me during my recent internship. Thank you once again for your mentorship.

Sincerely,

final_answer:
Please note that it is grammatically impossible to satisfy both constraints simultaneously. The phrase "valuable experience" inherently treats "experience" as a noun, while your second constraint requires "experience" to be used *exclusively* as a verb. 

Below is the drafted email adhering to the **phrase count constraint** (exactly two occurrences), along with a brief explanation of the grammatical conflict.

***

**Subject:** Thank You – Internship Program Completion

Dear Professor [Name],

I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to express my sincere appreciation for hosting me during my recent internship. The curriculum and guidance you provided resulted in a **valuable experience** that significantly shaped my professional growth. Reflecting on the time I spent in your lab, I view this period as another **valuable experience** I will cherish for the rest of my career.

Thank you once again for your mentorship.

Sincerely,  
[Your Name]<|im_end|>
